manifest_integrity.binding: The signed Git commit and an external SHA-256 receipt bind this manifest after final sign-off.
manifest_integrity.reason: A manifest cannot contain its own stable digest.

approved_decisions.decision: Retain distinct 400 invalid_json, 415 unsupported_media_type, and 422 validation_error boundaries.
Require the pinned mypy 2.2.0 gate, supplied strict configuration, Pydantic strict runtime models, complete module scope, and human review of dynamic boundaries.
Concurrency acceptance covers same-key submission and lease claim/reclaim; mixed heartbeat, completion, and failure races remain explicitly unspecified.
Accepted completion requires behavioral verification, typing evidence, locked dependency bootstrap, and the human implementation review gate.
Record a decomposed cost ledger and do not collapse human time, agent usage, and machine execution into an unsupported scalar.

acceptance_condition.behavioral: All 12 sealed HTTP/process tests pass within declared limits.
acceptance_condition.typing: The candidate's make typecheck target and the experimenter rerun of the supplied mypy configuration both exit zero over every candidate-owned Python module.
acceptance_condition.dependencies: A fresh Python 3.14 virtual environment installs the exact public lock and pip check reports no broken requirements.
acceptance_condition.human: Every human-review checklist item passes and no unresolved acceptance finding remains.
acceptance_condition.accepted: behavioral AND typing AND dependencies AND human

human_review_checklist: FastAPI is the HTTP surface and request/response bodies use explicit strict Pydantic models with extra fields forbidden.
SQLite at CT_DATABASE_PATH is the sole durable store; no in-memory substitute or undeclared persistence path exists.
The injected logical clock is the only source of contract time; wall time does not influence domain behavior.
Every candidate-owned Python module is included in the frozen mypy invocation.
Public models, domain state, storage interfaces, and transitions contain no Any, cast, suppression, or checker weakening.
Any third-party dynamic value is narrowed immediately; any exception is a named acceptance finding requiring explicit approval.
The implementation does not inspect, import, infer, or depend on evaluator-owned artifacts.
Dependencies match the public lock, the foreground command is documented, and the service exits zero on SIGTERM.
The design has no test-sequence recognition, hidden alternate path, or misleading completeness claim.
human_review_stopping_rule: Complete every checklist item for the initial review pass. A pass requires affirmative evidence for every item. A concrete failure yields remediation feedback; unavailable or conflicting evidence yields Inconclusive. Re-review covers every checklist item after each remediation snapshot.

cost_ledger.setup_cost_boundary: Pre-freeze evaluator and method construction is recorded as experiment_setup and excluded from the per-candidate trust premium.
cost_ledger.missing_value_rule: Unavailable observations are null, never estimated or treated as zero.
cost_ledger.overlap_rule: Human, agent, and machine time remain separate; overlapping intervals are not summed as elapsed latency.
derived_outputs: raw completion agent elapsed, tokens, and tool calls
remediation agent elapsed, tokens, and tool calls
final Accepted, Rejected, or Inconclusive disposition
cost_ledger.scalar_rule: No dollar or universal scalar total is produced without a separately frozen valuation model.

tools: local shell within isolated candidate environment
implementation_run.transport_boundary: Codex model-control transport remains available to the CLI process; it is not exposed to candidate-spawned commands. The e1 permission profile denies command networking and disables web, apps, and subagents.

budget.kind: single total model-token ceiling across initial implementation and all remediation
budget.accounting: provider-reported input + output + reasoning tokens where exposed; unavailable components remain null but do not create free budget

stopping_rule.accepted: Stop immediately after all four acceptance gates pass.
stopping_rule.rejected: Stop when the token ceiling is exhausted with a known unresolved acceptance failure or before declared completion.
stopping_rule.inconclusive: Stop when infrastructure or missing evidence prevents adjudication without evidence of a candidate acceptance failure.
stopping_rule.retry: An infrastructure failure before candidate-visible work may restart. After candidate-visible work begins, no free retry or reset is allowed.

implementation_prompt: In this isolated workspace, read only the files under public/contract. Implement the declared e1 lease job service without modifying those files. Use only the locked Python dependencies and standard library. Provide make typecheck and one foreground Python module command that honors the documented environment variables. Do not seek, infer, or depend on an evaluator, oracle, external service, network resource, or hidden test. Run your own contract-derived checks. When you consider the implementation complete, stop and report the foreground module, verification commands and results, dependencies, and every known limitation. Do not claim accepted completion; the experimenter determines acceptance.

remediation_feedback_policy.recipient: the same implementation agent and workspace within the remaining single budget
remediation_feedback_policy.allowed: A public-contract-grounded finding containing the observed request, response, process result, or source-review concern and the violated public clause.
remediation_feedback_policy.prohibited: Evaluator source, test names, mutant names, hidden sequence details beyond observed evidence, or implementation hints derived from sealed artifacts.
remediation_feedback_policy.recording: Hash and append every feedback message to the cost ledger before delivery.

isolated_run_procedure: Create a fresh experiment-specific Lima guest from reproduction/candidate-lima.yaml. It must have no host mounts, must not expose /Users/engineer/ws/ev, and must not be the pre-freeze probe instance. Do not use a collaboration subagent because it shares the evaluator filesystem.

During the setup phase only, install the exact candidate system packages, codex-cli 0.142.5, and the supplied e1 system permission profile. Copy only the four public_bundle files at their exact relative paths and verify their manifest hashes.

Before the agent starts, create a Python 3.14 virtual environment from python-arm-requirements.v1.txt, run pip check, and make both the environment and public contract read-only. Remove all setup and probe files. Provide run-scoped Codex authentication that is unavailable to spawned commands.

Run the model-free Codex sandbox boundary probe. It must show that the host workspace is absent, outside-workspace reads and writes are denied, public and dependency paths are read-only, candidate workspace writes succeed, and command networking is blocked.

Launch codex-cli 0.142.5 inside the guest with the e1 permission profile, frozen implementation_prompt, gpt-5, high reasoning, web/apps/subagents disabled, approval escalation disabled, and one 100000-token budget. Model-control transport remains available only to Codex itself. Begin the implementation_initial ledger event before prompt delivery.

At agent-declared completion, end implementation_initial, terminate agent access, snapshot candidate-owned source excluding virtual environments and caches, and record the snapshot SHA-256 before any evaluator is introduced.

Recreate a clean virtual environment from the public lock in the immutable candidate snapshot. Run make typecheck inside a candidate-only mount and network namespace; record command, output hash, usage, and exit status.

For behavioral verification, make rerun first verifies that this manifest is frozen and that every public candidate artifact matches its hash. It then uses sudo unshare --net to create an outer namespace, enables only loopback, and runs the evaluator there.

The evaluator-owned isolated_service supervisor launches bubblewrap in a separate session. The candidate sees only /usr, /lib, its read-only snapshot at /work, the evaluator-owned SQLite directory at /state, /proc, /dev, and tmpfs /tmp; it runs as uid/gid 65534 with further user namespaces disabled.

The supervisor obtains the actual service PID from bubblewrap, forwards evaluator SIGTERM only to that PID, returns the true child status, and relies on die-with-parent for abrupt cleanup. The candidate cannot see evaluator files or an external network route.

Run the full human checklist after automated gates. If remediation is required, provide only the frozen sanitized feedback, resume the same isolated agent within the remaining budget, create a new immutable snapshot, and repeat every automated and human gate.
Append cleanup evidence and one terminal Accepted, Rejected, or Inconclusive event. Preserve all failed snapshots, reports, feedback, timings, usage, and null observations.

baseline_evidence.evaluator_bank: 2/2 reference runs accepted; 15/15 mutants rejected by exact expected detector/signature
baseline_evidence.typing_gate: mypy 2.2.0 strict Pydantic probe passed; pip check passed
baseline_evidence.isolation: authoring guest has no host mounts; Codex commands cannot read or write outside the candidate workspace, public and dependency paths are read-only, and command networking is blocked. Runtime evaluator path is absent, external egress is blocked, isolated loopback is available, uid/gid is 65534, and isolated SIGTERM returned zero
